content.text: A **production-grade Netflix-style OTT streaming platform** built with Vite, React, Tailwind CSS, and a Node.js HLS backend. Authentication is fully serverless via **AWS Lambda + DynamoDB**. The platform is fully containerized with Docker Compose and deployed live on AWS EC2.

content.title: Live Platform
content.text: The platform is live at **ott.vpjoshi.in** — deployed on AWS EC2 with CloudFront CDN and Route 53.

content.caption: Homepage — Netflix-style browsing UI built with React and Tailwind CSS.
HLS adaptive bitrate streaming — player auto-switches quality based on bandwidth.

content.text: JoshiOTT solves three core problems — **efficient video transcoding**, **low-latency adaptive delivery**, and **secure serverless authentication** — without relying on expensive managed services like AWS MediaConvert or Cognito.

items: **Adaptive Bitrate HLS**: FFmpeg transcodes videos into 240p, 480p, and 720p renditions — player auto-selects based on bandwidth.
**Serverless Auth**: AWS Lambda handles login/signup — no always-on auth server needed.
**DynamoDB**: Scalable NoSQL storage for user accounts and session management.
**CloudFront CDN**: Static frontend served from AWS edge locations for minimal latency globally.
**Docker Compose**: Entire frontend + backend stack containerized and deployed on a single EC2 instance.
**JWT Protection**: Every video chunk request validated server-side — content inaccessible without a valid token.

content.title: Self-Hosted Advantage
content.text: By using **FFmpeg locally** instead of AWS MediaConvert, and Docker Compose instead of EKS, the platform achieves production-grade video delivery at a fraction of managed service costs.

content.caption: Figure 1: Full AWS architecture — Route 53 → CloudFront → EC2 with Docker Compose + Lambda auth.

items: ① **User visits** ott.vpjoshi.in → **Route 53** resolves DNS.
② **CloudFront CDN** serves the static React frontend from the nearest edge location.
③ **Login/Signup** requests hit **API Gateway → Lambda** which verifies credentials via **DynamoDB** and issues a **JWT**.
④ Authenticated requests (with JWT) hit the **Node.js HLS server** running on EC2.
⑤ Backend validates JWT → responds with **authorized HLS video chunks**.
⑥ Player receives the master playlist and selects the optimal quality rendition.

content.code:
# Simplified architecture
[User Browser]
       ↓
[React Frontend (Vite + Tailwind)] ↔ [AWS Lambda Auth] ↔ [DynamoDB]
       ↓
[Node.js HLS Server] — serves adaptive HLS streams
       ↓
[EC2 Instance with Docker Compose] — hosts frontend + backend containers

content.text: Raw video uploads are processed through an **FFmpeg transcoding pipeline** that converts them into HLS format with three quality renditions. The player monitors playback performance and switches quality dynamically.

content.code:
# FFmpeg HLS transcoding — multi-quality output
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 \
  -filter_complex "
    [0:v]split=3[v1][v2][v3];
    [v1]scale=1280:720[720p];
    [v2]scale=854:480[480p];
    [v3]scale=426:240[240p]
  " \
  -map [720p] -c:v libx264 -b:v 2800k -hls_time 6 -hls_playlist_type vod \
    -hls_segment_filename 'hls/720p_%03d.ts' hls/720p.m3u8 \
  -map [480p] -c:v libx264 -b:v 1400k -hls_time 6 -hls_playlist_type vod \
    -hls_segment_filename 'hls/480p_%03d.ts' hls/480p.m3u8 \
  -map [240p] -c:v libx264 -b:v 600k  -hls_time 6 -hls_playlist_type vod \
    -hls_segment_filename 'hls/240p_%03d.ts' hls/240p.m3u8

content.title: Adaptive Bitrate
content.text: The HLS player continuously monitors download speed and buffer health. If bandwidth drops, it seamlessly switches from 720p to 480p or 240p mid-stream — no buffering, no interruption.

Authentication is fully **serverless** — AWS Lambda handles all login and signup logic. User credentials and sessions are stored in **DynamoDB**. On successful auth, a **JWT is issued** and used to protect all subsequent API and video stream requests.

content.title: Chunk-Level Protection
content.text: JWT is validated on **every HLS segment request** — not just the player page. Direct chunk URL access without a valid token returns 401. Content cannot be scraped or downloaded by bypassing the UI.

The entire stack — React frontend and Node.js HLS backend — is containerized with **Docker Compose** and deployed on a single AWS EC2 instance. This keeps infrastructure simple and cost-efficient while maintaining full reproducibility.

content.code:
# docker-compose.yml
version: '3.8'
services:
  frontend:
    build: ./public
    ports:
      - '80:80'
    depends_on:
      - backend

  backend:
    build: ./src
    ports:
      - '3000:3000'
    environment:
      - JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}
      - AWS_REGION=${AWS_REGION}
      - DYNAMODB_TABLE=${DYNAMODB_TABLE}
    volumes:
      - ./hls:/app/hls

# Deploy on EC2
git clone https://github.com/Joshi-labs/joshiott_latest.git
cd joshiott_latest

# Install dependencies
cd public && npm install && npm run build
cd ../src && npm install

# Launch
cd ..
docker-compose up --build -d

# Access at
https://ott.vpjoshi.in

content.title: Prerequisites
content.text: Node.js v16+, Docker & Docker Compose, AWS credentials configured for Lambda and DynamoDB access, and SSH access to the EC2 instance.

items: **Vite** - Fast build tooling and dev server.
**React JS** - Component-based Netflix-style UI.
**Tailwind CSS** - Utility-first responsive styling.
**HLS.js** - Browser-side adaptive bitrate HLS player.
**Node JS + Express JS** - HLS stream server with JWT middleware.
**FFmpeg** - Video transcoding into 240p/480p/720p HLS renditions.
**JWT** - Stateless auth tokens protecting API and video chunks.
**Route 53** - DNS routing.
**CloudFront CDN** - Static asset delivery from edge locations.
**Lambda** - Serverless auth handler via API Gateway.
**DynamoDB** - NoSQL storage for users and sessions.
**EC2** - Hosts the Docker Compose stack.
**Docker** - Containerized frontend and backend.
**Docker Compose** - Multi-container orchestration on EC2.

content.code:
# Authentication (via AWS Lambda)
POST /api/auth/register     → Register user, store in DynamoDB, return JWT
POST /api/auth/login        → Verify credentials in DynamoDB, return JWT

# Content
GET  /api/content           → List all available titles
GET  /api/content/:id       → Get title metadata and thumbnail

# Streaming (JWT protected)
GET  /stream/:videoId/master.m3u8     → Master HLS playlist
GET  /stream/:videoId/:quality.m3u8  → Quality-specific playlist
GET  /stream/:videoId/:segment.ts    → Individual video chunk

# Watchlist (JWT protected)
GET  /api/watchlist         → Get user's saved titles
POST /api/watchlist/:id     → Add title to watchlist
DELETE /api/watchlist/:id   → Remove from watchlist
